title: Calycorectes minutifolius
intro: Calycorectes minutifolius is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as a shrub or small tree native to the Brazilian Atlantic Forest. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Calycorectes minutifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems.
taxonomy: Calycorectes minutifolius is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae. This family is commonly known as the myrtle family, and it includes a wide range of flowering plants. The Myrtaceae family is part of the order Myrtales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The order Myrtales is a part of the clade Rosids, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families of roses and other related species. Calycorectes minutifolius is native to the Americas.
